perturbation.original_text: 3.3 PAYMENT. All payments to PivX shall be made within thirty (30) days after the receipt by Detto of the PivX's invoice. Detto shall pay PivX a late charge on outstanding amounts due equal to one and one-half percent (1.50%) per month or the maximum amount allowed by law, whichever is less. All payments shall be made in United States Dollars, free of any withholding tax and of any currency control or other restrictions to PivX. PivX shall have the right, at reasonable times and on reasonable notice, to inspect and audit the books and records of Detto to verify the accuracy of any statements. In the event that such an inspection discloses any error of any amount, the parties shall by appropriate payment promptly adjust for the error. If Detto fails to make payments when due, PivX shall be entitled to, in its sole discretion, to take any one or more of the following: (a) place Detto on credit hold, in which case, PivX may cease to fulfill Detto's orders to any new Third Parties; (b) rescind Detto's right to sell or distribute any additional Qwik-Fix Pro or Documentation hereunder; and (c) to require that Detto direct all future payments, for licenses sold in the past or in the future, from the Third Parties directly to PivX or a lock box or an account designated by PivX for such purpose, to be applied by PivX to the payment default (and interest thereon) until such defaults have been satisfied. All amounts received by PivX hereunder shall be nonrefundable except for any payments received or held under PivX's control pursuant to clause "c" of the last sentence after Detto's payment default has been satisfied.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 PAYMENT. All payments to PivX shall be made within sixty (60) days after the receipt by Detto of the PivX's invoice. Detto shall pay PivX a late charge on outstanding amounts due equal to one and one-half percent (1.50%) per month or the maximum amount allowed by law, whichever is less. All payments shall be made in United States Dollars, free of any withholding tax and of any currency control or other restrictions to PivX. PivX shall have the right, at reasonable times and on reasonable notice, to inspect and audit the books and records of Detto to verify the accuracy of any statements. In the event that such an inspection discloses any error of any amount, the parties shall by appropriate payment promptly adjust for the error. If Detto fails to make payments when due, PivX shall be entitled to, in its sole discretion, to take any one or more of the following: (a) place Detto on credit hold, in which case, PivX may cease to fulfill Detto's orders to any new Third Parties; (b) rescind Detto's right to sell or distribute any additional Qwik-Fix Pro or Documentation hereunder; and (c) to require that Detto direct all future payments, for licenses sold in the past or in the future, from the Third Parties directly to PivX or a lock box or an account designated by PivX for such purpose, to be applied by PivX to the payment default (and interest thereon) until such defaults have been satisfied. All amounts received by PivX hereunder shall be nonrefundable except for any payments received or held under PivX's control pursuant to clause "c" of the last sentence after Detto's payment default has been satisfied. Notwithstanding the above, for the first three months of this agreement payments shall be made within 45 days.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment deadline from 30 to 60 days and then stating for the first three months the payment shall be made within 45 days. This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 3.3 because there are conflicting payment deadlines where the first sentence states 60 days and the last sentence states 45 days within the first three months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 PAYMENT. All payments to PivX shall be made within sixty (60) days after the receipt by Detto of the PivX's invoice. Detto shall pay PivX a late charge on outstanding amounts due equal to one and one-half percent (1.50%) per month or the maximum amount allowed by law, whichever is less. All payments shall be made in United States Dollars, free of any withholding tax and of any currency control or other restrictions to PivX. PivX shall have the right, at reasonable times and on reasonable notice, to inspect and audit the books and records of Detto to verify the accuracy of any statements. In the event that such an inspection discloses any error of any amount, the parties shall by appropriate payment promptly adjust for the error. If Detto fails to make payments when due, PivX shall be entitled to, in its sole discretion, to take any one or more of the following: (a) place Detto on credit hold, in which case, PivX may cease to fulfill Detto's orders to any new Third Parties; (b) rescind Detto's right to sell or distribute any additional Qwik-Fix Pro or Documentation hereunder; and (c) to require that Detto direct all future payments, for licenses sold in the past or in the future, from the Third Parties directly to PivX or a lock box or an account designated by PivX for such purpose, to be applied by PivX to the payment default (and interest thereon) until such defaults have been satisfied. All amounts received by PivX hereunder shall be nonrefundable except for any payments received or held under PivX's control pursuant to clause "c" of the last sentence after Detto's payment default has been satisfied. Notwithstanding the above, for the first three months of this agreement payments shall be made within 45 days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.3 introduces a contradiction. The first sentence states that 'All payments to PivX shall be made within sixty (60) days', but the added sentence, 'Notwithstanding the above, for the first three months of this agreement payments shall be made within 45 days,' directly contradicts this. This creates an in-text contradiction because the payment deadline is defined as both 60 days and 45 days for the first three months, making it unclear which deadline applies. Since the Changed text and Contradicted Text are in the same location, this creates a direct contradiction. This adheres to the definition of inconsistencies in In Text Contradiction where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 TERM OF AGREEMENT. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for six (6) months.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 TERM OF AGREEMENT. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for twelve (12) months. This agreement will automatically renew for another 6 months unless either party provides a 30 day notice to terminate before the end of the initial 12 month period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the contract term from 6 months to 12 months, but retaining the original termination clause referring to a 6 month term. This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 5.2, because Section 5.2 references Section 5.1 with the understanding that the term is 6 months, not 12, but the change to Section 5.1 alters that assumption
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 TERMINATION OF AGREEMENT. PivX may terminate this Agreement for convenience by giving at least thirty (30) days written notice of termination to Detto. This Agreement may be terminated by either party in the event of a material breach of this Agreement by the other party that is not cured within thirty (30) days of the other party's receipt of written notice of such breach. If a material breach is cured within a thirty (30) day cure period this Agreement shall remain in effect as if no material breach had occurred. This Agreement shall terminate automatically without notice and without further action by the other party in the event that the other party becomes insolvent, which means it becomes unable to pay its debts in the ordinary course of business as they come due, or makes an assignment of this Agreement for the benefit of creditors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the contract term from 6 months to 12 months and includes an automatic renewal for 6 months after the initial 12-month period. The original contradicted text in Section 5.2 makes no reference to the new term. This introduces a contradiction as Section 5.2 implies that the entire agreement will be for 6 months, and does not account for the changes in 5.1.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 NOTICES. All notices shall be given in writing and shall be considered effective when (a) personally delivered, (b) upon confirmed receipt if sent by electronic mail or facsimile; or (c) two (2) days after posting if sent by overnight registered private carrier (e.g. DHL, Federal Express, etc.).
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 NOTICES. All notices shall be given in writing and shall be considered effective when (a) personally delivered, (b) upon confirmed receipt if sent by electronic mail; or (c) five (5) days after posting if sent by standard mail. Facsimile is not considered a valid form of communication for contractual notices.
perturbation.explanation: Removing facsimile from the list of acceptable notification methods and changing the mail delivery timeframe, directly contradicts the previous notice clause. This creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction about how notices should be delivered to be contractually binding, specifically impacting other sections that refer to notice requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 NOTICES. All notices shall be given in writing and shall be considered effective when (a) personally delivered, (b) upon confirmed receipt if sent by electronic mail; or (c) five (5) days after posting if sent by standard mail. Facsimile is not considered a valid form of communication for contractual notices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.3 alters the acceptable methods and timeframes for delivering notices. Specifically, it removes facsimile as a valid method and changes the mail delivery timeframe, directly conflicting with the contradicted section which also outlines notice procedures. This creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction about how notices should be delivered to be contractually binding, creating an inconsistency as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
